gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Run a property test: generate `budget` inputs from `gen` and check `prop` for each."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns the first failure (shrunk to a minimal counterexample), `Skipped` if the generator"] # [doc = " cannot produce any input, or `Pass` if all trials succeed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (the verdict is an exact function of the run)"] # [doc = " A passing `for_all` **backs an `Empirical` claim** about the property — not `Proven`. The"] # [doc = " harness has no operation that turns \"passed N trials\" into `Proven`; that would be the"] # [doc = " exact VR-5 violation the module exists to prevent (spec §4 / §4.1 C2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Verdict::Fail{..}` — property violated; carries shrunk counterexample + seed."] # [doc = " - `Verdict::Skipped{SkipReason::NeedsRecord}` — generator produced no inputs."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none (pure; seeded — C6)"] # [doc = " The `seed` parameter makes the run reproducible (RT3). No undeclared entropy is drawn."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " A `Fail` verdict carries the shrunk counterexample description + the reproducing seed so"] # [doc = " the failure can be reproduced and explained (C3/G11/SC-3)."] pub fn for_all < T , G > (gen : & mut G , seed : u64 , budget : Budget , prop : impl Fn (& T) -> bool) -> Verdict where G : Gen < T > , T : core :: fmt :: Debug , { let mut rng = Rng :: new (seed) ; let mut generated_any = false ; for trial in 0 .. budget . trials () { let Some (input) = gen . generate (& mut rng) else { if ! generated_any { return Verdict :: Skipped { reason : SkipReason :: NeedsRecord , } ; } break ; } ; generated_any = true ; if ! prop (& input) { let shrunk_desc = shrink_to_minimal (& input , & mut | v | ! prop (v) , gen , trial) ; return Verdict :: Fail { record : FailRecord { description : shrunk_desc , seed , trial , context : "for_all property violated" . to_owned () , } , } ; } } if ! generated_any { return Verdict :: Skipped { reason : SkipReason :: NeedsRecord , } ; } Verdict :: Pass }
gaps.reason: a `where` clause has no Mycelium equivalent

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Shrink a failing value to a minimal counterexample."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Tries shrink candidates from the generator; returns a `Debug` string of the minimal"] # [doc = " reproducing input. The number of shrink steps is bounded (at most 1000) to keep the"] # [doc = " shrinking itself bounded (C6)."] fn shrink_to_minimal < T , G > (initial : & T , still_fails : & mut impl FnMut (& T) -> bool , gen : & mut G , trial : u32 ,) -> String where G : Gen < T > , T : core :: fmt :: Debug , { let mut best = format ! ("{initial:?}") ; let mut candidates = gen . shrink (initial) ; let mut steps = 0usize ; while ! candidates . is_empty () && steps < 1000 { let mut next_candidates = vec ! [] ; for c in & candidates { if still_fails (c) { best = format ! ("{c:?}") ; next_candidates = gen . shrink (c) ; break ; } } candidates = next_candidates ; steps += 1 ; } format ! ("trial={trial} value={best}") }
gaps.reason: a `where` clause has no Mycelium equivalent

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Run a golden / snapshot test: compare `produced` against the stored baseline."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (the verdict is an exact function of produced vs baseline)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Verdict::Fail{..}` — mismatch; carries a diff (C3 EXPLAIN: the diff + context)."] # [doc = " - `Verdict::Skipped{SkipReason::NeedsRecord}` — baseline is `None` (missing)."] # [doc = "   A missing baseline is **never** auto-accepted (C1/G2 — the honesty crux for golden tests)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects"] # [doc = " Declared: none at this layer (the baseline is passed in, not read from disk). The runner/CI"] # [doc = " wiring that supplies the baseline owns any filesystem IO — this library owns only the"] # [doc = " comparison and verdict."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " A mismatch carries both the expected and produced values so the failure is inspectable (C3)."] pub fn golden (baseline : Option < & GoldenBaseline > , name : & str , produced : & str) -> Verdict { match baseline { None => { Verdict :: Skipped { reason : SkipReason :: NeedsRecord , } } Some (b) if b . name != name => { Verdict :: Skipped { reason : SkipReason :: NeedsRecord , } } Some (b) => { if b . expected == produced { Verdict :: Pass } else { let diff = make_diff (& b . expected , produced) ; Verdict :: Fail { record : FailRecord { description : format ! ("golden mismatch for '{}': expected {:?}, got {:?}; diff: {}" , name , b . expected , produced , diff) , seed : 0 , trial : 0 , context : format ! ("golden({})" , name) , } , } } } } }

`let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Run a differential (oracle) test: require `lhs(input) == rhs(input)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This implements the M-151/M-210 interp↔AOT/native oracle pattern (NFR-7). The two-level"] # [doc = " agreement bar (§8-Q5 RESOLVED): observable-result equivalence is the floor enforced here;"] # [doc = " the tag/EXPLAIN equivalence level requires deeper `std.diag` call-site integration (FLAG-Q5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (the verdict is an exact function of both outputs)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Verdict::Fail{..}` — disagreement; carries both outputs for inspection (C3/EXPLAIN)."] # [doc = " - `Verdict::Skipped{SkipReason::BackendUnavailable}` — `lhs` or `rhs` is unavailable."] # [doc = "   An unavailable backend is **never** a silent pass (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none (pure, given available backends — C6)"] # [doc = " Per-backend IO declared by the `available` flags."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " A `Fail` carries both outputs and the input description so the disagreement is inspectable"] # [doc = " (C3/G11/SC-3). **FLAG-Q5:** the tag/EXPLAIN level of the §8-Q5 two-level bar awaits"] # [doc = " `std.diag` (FLAG-DIAG)."] pub fn differential < O > (input_desc : & str , lhs_available : bool , lhs : impl FnOnce () -> O , rhs_available : bool , rhs : impl FnOnce () -> O ,) -> Verdict where O : PartialEq + core :: fmt :: Debug , { if ! lhs_available || ! rhs_available { return Verdict :: Skipped { reason : SkipReason :: BackendUnavailable , } ; } let lhs_out = lhs () ; let rhs_out = rhs () ; if lhs_out == rhs_out { Verdict :: Pass } else { Verdict :: Fail { record : FailRecord { description : format ! ("differential disagreement for input '{input_desc}': lhs={lhs_out:?} rhs={rhs_out:?}") , seed : 0 , trial : 0 , context : format ! ("differential({})" , input_desc) , } , } } }
gaps.reason: a `where` clause has no Mycelium equivalent

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Aggregate a slice of verdicts into a [`Summary`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Skipped` and `Undetermined` counts are **kept distinct** from `Pass` (the crux: \"green\""] # [doc = " cannot silently include \"did not check\" — C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (a total function over verdicts — no approximation)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN: yes — the per-class counts are the inspection artifact (spec §4)"] pub fn summarize (vs : & [Verdict]) -> Summary { let mut passed = 0u32 ; let mut failed = 0u32 ; let mut skipped = 0u32 ; let mut undetermined = 0u32 ; for v in vs { match v { Verdict :: Pass => passed += 1 , Verdict :: Fail { .. } => failed += 1 , Verdict :: Skipped { .. } => skipped += 1 , Verdict :: Undetermined { .. } => undetermined += 1 , } } Summary { passed , failed , skipped , undetermined , } }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " True only if there are no failures **and** skipped/undetermined counts are surfaced (i.e.,"] # [doc = " the caller can see them — this function does NOT hide them)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " \"Green\" means *checked and passed*, never *did not check* (C1/G2 — the honesty crux)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN: yes — the caller can inspect `Summary` to see the skip/undetermined counts"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Note:** `is_green` returns `true` when `failed == 0`, regardless of skipped/undetermined"] # [doc = " count. The skipped/undetermined are *surfaced* in the `Summary` — `is_green` does not hide"] # [doc = " them, but it does not force them to block a pass. This matches the spec's \"skips are surfaced,"] # [doc = " not silently absent\" intent: a skip is visible in the Summary and must be explained; whether"] # [doc = " it blocks the CI run is the runner's decision, not the harness's. An `is_green` that returned"] # [doc = " `false` for any non-zero skip would violate C1 by treating \"could not run\" as \"failed\"."] pub fn is_green (s : & Summary) -> bool { s . failed == 0 }
gaps.reason: field access on a non-`self` base — the transpiler tracks no local types, so the projection cannot be resolved to a constructor position (only `self.<field>` desugars to a `match`)
